title: The 13 billion years that led to us – a cosmological timelapse | Aeon Videos
text:
The 13 billion years that led to us – a cosmological timelapseThe US musician and filmmaker John D Boswell (aka melodysheep) is known for his cinematic explorations of the cosmos, which combine 3D renderings of deep space with original electronic compositions. In Timelapse of the Universe, he takes viewers on a voyage from the birth of the Universe to the present day, with humanity’s existence occupying only a brief flash at the very end of the timeline. While the information presented – from the formation of galaxies to the birth of black holes – is well-trodden territory for the cosmos-curious, Boswell’s presentation, which condenses some 13 billion years into 10 epic minutes, nonetheless makes for a deeply absorbing refresher.Video by melodysheep29 June 2026GooglePREFER AEON ON GOOGLEvideoCosmologyDeep time and beyond: the great nothingness at the end of the Universe29 minutesvideoPhysicsHow would a piano sound on Mars?
